description: Canonical social-proof template library for Reach Social ad generation.

global_render_principles: Templates must remain legible and compelling on mobile first.
Social proof should be visually obvious within the first glance.
A product or offer should always be visually anchored somewhere in the composition unless explicitly proof-only.
Video behavior must preserve readability and prioritize one primary motion zone.
All templates must degrade gracefully to image-only mode if video is unavailable.

templates.name: Testimonial Spotlight
templates.tagline: One strong quote. One clear product. One clean conversion story.
templates.purpose: Highlight a single high-quality testimonial as the hero proof element, supported by product, rating, and review count.
templates.summary: A quote-first template designed to make one customer testimonial feel premium, credible, and conversion-oriented.

supported_entry_points.behavior: Preferred entry point. Product is primary and testimonial supports conversion.
Allowed when a testimonial or quote can be derived from UGC, review, or creator text.

ideal_use_cases: hero product ads with one standout review

copy_strategy.primary_copy_role: testimonial text is the hero message

video_support.primary_video_role: product hero motion or soft background motion
rules: Quote card remains pinned and legible over motion.
Only one primary video area should animate prominently.
Background motion must be softened with overlay treatment.

selection_rules: Prefer this template when one testimonial is clearly stronger than all others.
Prefer this template when product trust and clarity matter more than creator personality.
Do not use as default when the proof is mainly engagement-driven or creator-led.

templates.name: UGC Split-Screen
templates.tagline: Real content on one side. Product conversion on the other.
templates.purpose: Pair a creator or UGC asset with the product in a split composition that makes the proof feel authentic and shoppable.
templates.summary: A dual-panel template where social proof comes from a creator, customer, or post asset, balanced against a product panel.

supported_entry_points.behavior: Best fit when starting from a branded or UGC post.
Allowed if a matched creator/lifestyle/UGC asset is available or can be resolved.

supported_post_types.behavior: Can use branded post as the social panel.
Best fit. UGC permissions state matters operationally, but the template concept remains the same.

copy_strategy.headline_role: secondary support to the quote or caption
copy_strategy.primary_copy_role: UGC quote or caption drives the message

rules: If both UGC and product have video, only one should autoplay prominently.
The quote/caption overlay must stay readable over motion.
The composition should still work as an image-only split layout.

selection_rules: Prefer this template when the creative should feel native to social.
Prefer this template when a creator, customer, or post asset is a major strength.
Do not use when there is no viable companion social asset.

templates.name: Review Collage
templates.tagline: Many voices. One product. Dense, convincing proof.
templates.purpose: Surround a hero product with multiple reviews or review snippets to create a high-density social-proof composition.
templates.summary: A review-rich template that makes the product feel validated by many customers at once.

supported_entry_points.behavior: Preferred entry point. Product remains the center of gravity.
Allowed if the system can aggregate relevant review or testimonial content around the product.

copy_strategy.primary_copy_role: review card system is the main copy experience

rules: Review cards remain static and readable.
Motion should be confined to the hero media area.
If no video exists, layout remains fully viable as a static collage.

selection_rules: Prefer this template when multiple short reviews are available.
Prefer this template when the proof comes from consensus rather than one hero quote.
Do not use when only one meaningful proof statement exists.

templates.name: Results Proof
templates.tagline: Lead with measurable outcomes and back them with trust.
templates.purpose: Turn ratings, performance metrics, customer counts, or outcome claims into the main persuasive device.
templates.summary: A metrics-first template where proof is driven by measurable outcomes rather than purely emotional testimonials.

supported_entry_points.behavior: Preferred when product-level metrics or trust data are available.
Allowed when post or campaign data can support a performance narrative.

copy_strategy.headline_role: states the main result or promise
copy_strategy.primary_copy_role: metrics are the main proof language

rules: Metrics remain stable and readable over motion.
Motion should reinforce the proof narrative, not distract from it.
Metric cards can animate subtly, but the numeric content must remain clear.

selection_rules: Prefer this template when quantitative proof is the strongest asset.
Prefer this template when the campaign needs authority and clarity.
Do not use when the only usable proof is emotional or creator-led.

templates.name: Testimonial Overlay
templates.tagline: The image is the ad. Logo, headline, product, CTA, and quote sit on top in safe zones.
templates.purpose: Image-as-canvas variant of testimonial_spotlight. The hero photo fills the entire frame; required elements (logo, headline, product+rating, CTA) are placed dynamically in subject-safe regions using the detect pipeline's overlay-zone analysis. Quote is optional — drops if no safe room remains.
templates.summary: Renders the full image as the canvas and overlays elements at runtime in safe zones determined per image. No fixed canvas zones; placement is computed from each image's restrictions/density/brightness analysis.

supported_entry_points.behavior: Best fit when starting from a creator/UGC post — the image dominates and is the emotional hook.
Allowed when product hero imagery is strong enough to carry the frame.

ideal_use_cases: creator UGC product ads where the image is the emotional hook
any media where the image already tells the story and overlays add commerce signals

copy_strategy.primary_copy_role: headline drives, quote supplements when present

rules: All overlays must remain pinned and legible while the bg plays.
Adaptive scrim required behind text on bright/busy bg regions.
Image-only mode is the canonical fallback — video is decorative.

selection_rules: Prefer when the image is already a strong creative — no need to compose around it.
Prefer for UGC / creator media where authenticity matters more than information density.
Skip when the image is poor quality or the subject occupies the entire frame leaving no safe overlay area (renderer auto-falls back to inset mode in that case).
distinctive_features: no fixed canvas zones — placement computed per image
10% subject overlap budget for non-product restrictions
adaptive scrim strength based on bg/text contrast gap
inset fallback when no safe overlay area exists

templates.name: Product Overlay
templates.tagline: The image is the ad. Logo, headline, product card (image + name + price), and CTA sit on top in safe zones.
templates.purpose: Image-as-canvas overlay focused on a specific product. The hero photo fills the entire frame; logo, headline, a product card (catalog image + name + price), and CTA are placed in subject-safe regions using the detect pipeline's overlay-zone analysis.
templates.summary: Product-focused sibling of testimonial_overlay. Renders the full image as the canvas and overlays the product card + CTA at runtime in safe zones.

supported_entry_points.behavior: Best fit when starting from a creator/UGC post — the image dominates and is the emotional hook.
Allowed when product hero imagery is strong enough to carry the frame.

ideal_use_cases: creator UGC product ads where the image is the emotional hook
any media where the image already tells the story and overlays add commerce signals

copy_strategy.primary_copy_role: headline drives, quote supplements when present

rules: All overlays must remain pinned and legible while the bg plays.
Adaptive scrim required behind text on bright/busy bg regions.
Image-only mode is the canonical fallback — video is decorative.

selection_rules: Prefer when the image is already a strong creative — no need to compose around it.
Prefer for UGC / creator media where authenticity matters more than information density.
Skip when the image is poor quality or the subject occupies the entire frame leaving no safe overlay area (renderer auto-falls back to inset mode in that case).
distinctive_features: no fixed canvas zones — placement computed per image
10% subject overlap budget for non-product restrictions
adaptive scrim strength based on bg/text contrast gap
inset fallback when no safe overlay area exists

templates.name: Creator Endorsement
templates.tagline: A trusted face recommending a product with clear social validation.
templates.purpose: Make the creator or endorser the hero while still grounding the composition in product and trust signals.
templates.summary: A creator-first template where the recommendation itself is the core proof mechanism.

supported_entry_points.behavior: Preferred when starting from a creator, influencer, or social post.
Allowed if a creator asset or matched post exists.

ideal_use_cases: expert pick or favorite product ads

copy_strategy.primary_copy_role: creator quote is the message anchor

rules: Creator motion is the primary storytelling engine.
Product motion, if present, should be secondary or inset.
Quote and CTA remain pinned and legible while creator media plays.

selection_rules: Prefer this template when a creator face or identity is a major asset.
Prefer this template when endorsement quality is stronger than raw review density.
Do not use when no credible creator or human focal asset exists.
